note: SYNTHESIZED from ST 1.18.0 source, not captured. When two prompt objects share an identifier, ST resolves it FIRST-match: getPromptById uses `.find` (PromptManager.js:1257), so the first definition wins and the second is never emitted. RPT previously kept the last (Map overwrite); now first. Adapter-verify at Phase-2 wiring. Prose is RPT-authored + scrambled.

input.description: Two prompt objects both use identifier 'custom_x'; the first in the prompts array wins, the second is dropped.

prompts.name: Main
prompts.content: Vorne mandate: keep the grennel count.

prompts.name: Custom X (first)
prompts.content: FIRST-WINS: the copper tally is authoritative.

prompts.name: Custom X (second)
prompts.content: SECOND-LOSES: the tin tally must be ignored.

character.name: Tolm
character.description: Character brindle: Tolm, keeper of two conflicting ledgers.

chatMessages.content: Tolm, which tally do we trust?

mustContain: FIRST-WINS: the copper tally is authoritative.
mustNotContain: SECOND-LOSES: the tin tally must be ignored.
